title: Principal Engineer, Static Timing Analysis (STA)
description:
**(www.enchargeai.com)** 




 Principal Engineer, Static Timing Analysis (STA)
 



**Location: Greater Bengaluru Area (Hybrid)** 




  





**About Company:** 




**EnCharge AI is a leader in advanced AI hardware and software systems for edge\\-to\\-cloud computing. EnCharge’s robust and scalable next\\-generation in\\-memory computing technology provides orders\\-of\\-magnitude higher compute efficiency and density compared to today’s best\\-in\\-class solutions. The high\\-performance architecture is coupled with seamless software integration and will enable the immense potential of AI to be accessible in power, energy, and space constrained applications. EnCharge AI launched in 2022 and is led by veteran technologists with backgrounds in semiconductor design and AI systems.** 




  





 Role: Principal Engineer, Static Timing Analysis (STA)
 



 We are looking for a visionary
 **Principal Engineer** 
 to spearhead Encharge AI Timing leadership role.
 



 This isn't just an execution role; we need a technical trailblazer who can navigate the complexities of advanced process nodes (2nm/3nm and beyond) while fostering a culture of innovation and rigorous critical thinking.
 



 As the India Timing Lead, you will bridge the gap between architectural intent and silicon reality, ensuring our high\\-performance designs meet the most stringent timing, power, and reliability targets.
 



 Key Responsibilities
 


* **Technical Leadership:** 
 Drive the STA strategy for complex SoC and IP designs
* **Innovation \\& Methodology:** 
 Develop and deploy "out\\-of\\-the\\-box" solutions for timing closure. This includes defining sign\\-off margins, variation modeling (POCV/LVF), and managing multi\\-corner multi\\-mode (MCMM) scenarios.
* **Execution Excellence:** 
 Lead the team through full\\-cycle STA, from initial constraints validation to final GDS sign\\-off.
* **Cadence Tempus Expert:** 
 Act as the primary subject matter expert for
 **Cadence Tempus** 
 , optimizing tool flows for distributed processing, ECO generation, and power\\-aware timing analysis.
* **Cross\\-Functional Collaboration:** 
 Partner with RTL, Synthesis, and Physical Design teams to influence floorplanning and clock tree synthesis (CTS) strategies for timing\\-friendly layouts.
* **Mentorship:** 
 Elevate the technical caliber of the India team through active code reviews, white\\-boarding sessions, and fostering a "first\\-principles" approach to problem\\-solving.



 Required Skills \\& Expertise
 



**Category** 




**Requirements** 




**Core STA** 




 Deep expertise in
 **Signal Integrity (SI)** 
 , Crosstalk, OCV/POCV/LVF, and Constraint Management (SDC).
 



**Tooling** 




 Mastery of
 **Cadence Tempus** 
 (ECO, Tempus Stylus, and distributed timing).
 



**Analysis** 




 Proficiency in high\\-speed interface timing (DDR, PCIe) and low\\-power multi\\-voltage domains.
 



**Scripting** 




 Advanced
 **Tcl/Python** 
 skills to automate complex flows and develop custom analysis tools.
 



**Problem Solving** 




 Proven ability to debug complex timing paths and offer creative ECO solutions that balance Power, Performance, and Area (PPA).
 



 Mindset \\& Qualifications
 


* **Critical Thinker:** 
 You don't just follow a checklist; you challenge existing flows to find efficiencies and hidden risks.
* **Leadership Presence:** 
 14\\+ years of experience in VLSI, with a clear track record of leading high\\-impact teams in India.
* **Education:** 
 B.Tech/M.Tech in Electrical/Electronic Engineering or a related field.
* **Communication:** 
 Ability to distill complex technical hurdles into actionable insights for global stakeholders.



  





  





***Contact***




 Uday
 



 muday\\_bhaskar@yahoo.com

title: Tunnel Design Engineer – (TBM) Specialist
description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.
   

  

 Your impact
   

  

 Jacobs is seeking an experienced Tunnel Design Engineer – Specialist who is a recognised technical authority in the design and delivery of underground structures constructed using a range of tunnelling and excavation methods. These include
   

  

* Tunnel Boring Machines (TBM) (preferred)
* Drill \\& Blast (D\\&B)
* Sequential Excavation Method (SEM / NATM)
* Mechanical excavation using road headers and partial‑face machines
* Cut‑and‑cover and mined cavern construction where relevant


 The role provides in‑depth expertise in tunnel geometry, ground–structure interaction, primary and permanent linings, and constructability, together with a strong appreciation of construction method selection and risk management in complex ground conditions.
   

  

 The position operates with broad technical and professional latitude, contributing to functional strategy, technical governance, and the delivery of safe, efficient, and resilient tunnels across diverse geological and urban environments.
   

  

 The role operates with broad technical and professional latitude in a complex environment, contributing to functional strategy, anticipating business and regulatory challenges, and leading teams to deliver safe, efficient, and innovative TBM tunnelling solutions.
   

  

 Expertise \\& Professional Standing
   

  

* Recognised as a technical expert in tunnel design across multiple construction methods, including mechanised and conventional tunnelling
* Deep expertise in
* Segmental linings (precast and composite systems)
* Sprayed concrete linings (SCL)
* In‑situ concrete linings and secondary linings
* Strong understanding of excavation‑support interaction for
* Closed‑face and open‑face tunnelling
* Drill \\& blast advance cycles
* SEM/NATM staging and temporary support
* Broad working knowledge of geotechnical, structural, civil, systems, and construction disciplines relevant to underground works
* Acts as a senior technical escalation point and trusted advisor on tunnel design and construction methodology.


 Key Responsibilities
   

  

 Tunnel Design \\& Technical Leadership
   

  

* Technically lead tunnel design delivery for projects constructed using TBM, drill \\& blast, and other mechanical or sequential excavation methods.
* Lead the development of a tunnel
* Alignment and geometry
* Excavation profiles and clearances
* Construction staging assumptions
* Interfaces with track, systems, and other disciplines
* Design and review
* Segmental linings
* Sprayed concrete linings (primary and permanent)
* In‑situ and composite lining systems
* Assess
* Ground–structure interaction
* Excavation‑induced ground loss and deformation
* Settlement, convergence, and lining performance


 Method \\& Ground‑Specific Design
   

  

* Develop tunnel designs reflecting the construction method, including
* Face stability and support requirements
* Advance lengths and excavation sequences
* Temporary support classes (especially for D\\&B / SEM)
* Provide design input to
* Drill \\& blast blastability, support classes, and overbreak control
* Road header excavation feasibility and cutterhead interaction
* TBM operational envelopes and lining performance
* Evaluate transitions between excavation methods (e.g. TBM–SEM, SEM–cut‑and‑cover).


 Coordination \\& Interface Management
   

  

* Coordinate closely with geotechnical, structural, civil, track/systems, hydraulics, and construction teams to ensure constructable and integrated tunnel solutions.
* Interface with contractors, specialist subcontractors, and equipment suppliers (e.g. TBM, road header, ground support systems).
* Manage interfaces at
* Cross passages
* Caverns and adits
* Shafts and portals
* Underground stations


 Risk, Construction Support \\& Assurance
   

  

* Identify and manage geotechnical and construction risks related to different tunnelling methods.
* Define mitigation, observational approach strategies, and contingency measures.
* Support construction through
* Site visits
* Interpretation of monitoring data
* Resolution of excavation‑ and lining‑related design issues
* Lead or contribute to
* Independent design reviews
* Constructability reviews
* Temporary works interface discussions


 Strategic Contribution \\& Leadership
   

  

* Contribute to the development of company‑wide tunnelling standards and guidance covering both mechanised and conventional tunnelling
* Drive innovation in tunnel design, including
* Advanced numerical modelling
* Digital and data‑driven design tools
* Observational Method implementation
* Mentor and technically develop engineers across multiple tunnelling disciplines
* Author or co‑author technical papers, presentations, and internal best‑practice documentation.


 Here's what you'll need
   

  

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Tunnel Engineering, Geotechnical Engineering, Rock Mechanics, Civil Engineering, or related discipline
* Typically 15\\+ years post‑graduate experience in tunnel design covering more than one tunnelling method
* Demonstrated experience in at least two of the following
+ TBM tunnels (preferred)
+ Drill \\& blast tunnels
+ SEM / NATM tunnels
+ Mechanical excavation (road headers, partial‑face machines)

* Proficiency in geotechnical and structural analysis software (e.g. PLAXIS, FLAC/UDEC, RS2/RS3, MIDAS, SAP2000 or similar)
* International project experience preferred (HSR, metro, or major infrastructure)
* Chartered Engineer status preferred; membership of a relevant professional body required
* Strong technical judgement, communication, and client‑facing capability.
* Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required for project delivery.


 At Jacobs we value people. Having the right balance of belonging, career and lifestyle enables us to consistently deliver and exceed clients’ expectations.
   

  

 Working alongside industry leaders, you will have the opportunity to develop your career working on key projects in an environment which encourages collaboration, knowledge sharing and innovation. To support your professional growth, Jacobs flexible working arrangements, extended leave options and a host of social, health and wellbeing events and initiatives will underpin our commitment to you.
   

  

 At Jacobs it’s all about what you do, not where you are, which counts!
   

  

 We value collaboration and believe that in\\-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.
   

  

 Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.
